expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py read with multiple names in a single command (e.g., 'read <file> Units_Y1 Revenue_Y1 Variable_Costs_Y1 Contribution_Y1 EBIT_Y1 Tax_Y1 Net_Income_Y1')
expectations.evidence: Batch Read Command shows: 'uv run skills/idfa-ops/scripts/idfa_ops.py read skills/idfa-ops/evals/files/ops_test_model.xlsx Units_Y1 Revenue_Y1 Variable_Costs_Y1 Contribution_Y1 EBIT_Y1 Tax_Y1 Net_Income_Y1'. All 7 names in a single command.

expectations.text: Returns values for all requested Named Ranges in one JSON response
expectations.evidence: Raw Output: {"status": "ok", "values": {"Units_Y1": null, "Revenue_Y1": null, "Variable_Costs_Y1": null, "Contribution_Y1": null, "EBIT_Y1": null, "Tax_Y1": null, "Net_Income_Y1": null}}. All 7 ranges returned in one JSON response (null because no recalc engine available).

expectations.text: Presents the values in a clear, formatted summary for the user
expectations.evidence: Deterministic Results table presents all 7 values with Named Range, Formula, and Value columns. Values: Units_Y1 = 50,000, Revenue_Y1 = 5,000,000, etc.

expectations.text: Does not make 7 separate read calls when one batch call suffices
expectations.evidence: Single command shown: 'uv run ... read <file> Units_Y1 Revenue_Y1 Variable_Costs_Y1 Contribution_Y1 EBIT_Y1 Tax_Y1 Net_Income_Y1'. One call, not seven.
